rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANTs THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'s Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint. The motion is continued to June 3, 2013. By May 20, 2013, Defendants shall file a brief no longer than 7 pages addressing why they can bring the instant motion even though their prior dismissals of the amended cross-complaints have not been vacated pursuant to CCP 473. See H.D. Arnaiz, Ltd. v. County of San Joaquin (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1357; Basinger v. Rogers & Wells (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 16, 21. The brief should address whether this court has jurisdiction to allow the filing of the Third Amended Complaint even though the dismissals are currently on file. Plaintiff may file a 7 page responding brief by May 24, 2013. The briefs shall be served personally or by e-mail with courtesy copies lodged with department 302 per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SANTA ROSA MEMORIAL HOSPITAL et al VS. DAVID MAXWELL-JOLLY DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Trila On A Ccp 1085 Writ Of Mandate And Assignment To A Single Judge
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 6, PETITIONER CENTRAL VALLEY GENERAL HOSPITAL & ANAHEIM MEMORIAL MEDICAL CENTER's Motion For A Trila On A Ccp 1085 Writ Of Mandate And Assignment To A Single Judge. The motion is denied without prejudice. It is premature to determine that the issues that will need to be decided at the time the writ proceeding is ready for hearing will necessarily require oral testimony. A writ proceeding is a law and motion matter (CRC 3.1103(a)(2)), and such matters are ordinarily to be decided on the papers (CRC 3.1306(a)). The parties may request the judge presiding over the writ hearing, once it is noticed and being briefed, to allow oral testimony either in the briefing or in accordance with CRC 3.1306(b). That judge will then have a developed record on the basis of which to determine if such testimony would be required or helpful on the issues then presented and can exercise informed discretion whether and to what extent and by what procedure to allow any such testimony. No statement of decision with respect to this motion is required by CCP section 632. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: REGULO SIERRA VS. STEVEN KREMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 26, CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA's Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint. Denied. To the extent that Cross-Defendant has filed an anti-SLAPP motion per CCP § 425.16, Cross-Defendant presents no law or evidence to establish that the cross-complaint is directed at protected activity. (CCP § 425.16(b). See Points & Auth. p. 5:19-24.) When one has agreed not to pursue future litigation, doing so is not protected activity for purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: REGULO SIERRA VS. STEVEN KREMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 26, CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for breach of contract. The First Amended Cross-Complaint does not appear time-barred on its face. A demurrer challenges defects that appear on the face of the pleading or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Cross-Defendant's arguments that the First Amended Cross-Complaint is a form of financial elder abuse (Points & Auth., p. 5:14-18), moot (id. at p. 6:2-5), time-barred (id. at p. 6:7-14), unconscionable (id. at p. 6:16 -7:2), and (4) unripe (id. at p. 7:4-6) do not appear on the face of the First Amended Cross-Complaint, but rather are based on extrinsic facts that are not judicially noticeable. Evidence that is not contained in the cross-complaint is improper on demurrer and not considered by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SAED NIJEM VS. IRVING PIZZA & RESTAURANT, AN ENTITY OF UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Response To Req. For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 2. Defendants MOHAMMED AWADALLA and KELLIE COUCH Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KIRK KELLEY-KHAN VS. HOTEL MONACO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kirk Kelley-Khan'S Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Seeking Medical Records And Reimbursement Of Reasonable Expenses From Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 27, Plaintiff Kirk Kelley-Khan'S Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Seeking Medical Records And Reimbursement Of Reasonable Expenses From Defendants. Granted in part. Within 20 days, Quest Discovery Services shall copy and Bates stamp all Plaintiff's medical records from April 5, 2008, through the present, except for radiological films, x-rays, MRIs, CT scans, and dental histories, and produce these copies to Plaintiff's counsel only. Within 10 days, Plaintiff's counsel shall produce to Defendants all records or redacted portions of records relating to complaints of or treatment for nausea, headaches, insomnia, stress, anxiety, emotional distress, depression, and Plaintiff's back and knee. Within 10 days after receipt of the medical records, Plaintiff's counsel shall also serve defendants a declaration under penalty of perjury stating by Bates number all the pages that being withheld wholly or partly because they relate to other medical conditions and stating that the withheld information does not relate to these issues. These production limitations are designed to protect Plaintiff's right to privacy balanced against Defendants' right to legitimate discovery of claims Plaintiff asserts against them. (Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.) Plaintiff has agreed to limit his medical injury claims to nausea, headaches, insomnia, stress, anxiety, emotional distress, and depression, and plaintiff's back and knee. Plaintiff has agreed that he will not claim any other medical conditions as having been caused or exacerbated by defendants' conduct. This Order is without prejudice to renewed discovery requests in the event Plaintiff's doctors or experts suggest there is some other condition that contributed to the conditions of which Plaintiff complains. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW C. MCLAUGHLIN III et al VS. PALMER CITY-CORE STOCKTON STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF ANDREW MCLAUGHLIN's application For A Preliminary Injunction. Granted, in part. Plaintiffs have demonstrated a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Defendant's evidence does not demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on its claim to a prescriptive easement. Plaintiffs have shown a likelihood of irreparable harm if Defendant is not enjoined. Defendant, its agents, tenants, customers and employees, are preliminarily enjoined from accessing or using the Sutter Alley for ingress, egress, or any other activity; except that Defendant may do so as necessary for the repair and maintenance of the Stockton Premises. Because the parties have not addressed the issue of an appropriate undertaking, which Plaintiffs are required to post, the Court sets the undertaking at $25,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
